key_insight: Plasma-sprayed nano Al2O3–TiO2 coatings improved corrosion resistance on AISI 1020 steel, with 4 wt.% Al2O3 + 2 wt.% TiO2 giving the best protection.
engineering_relevance: The study identifies coating composition and NaCl environment as the main drivers of corrosion performance, helping optimize thermal-spray coatings for harsher service conditions.
method: Definitive Screening Design (DSD) varied Al2O3 content, NaCl concentration, exposure time, temperature, and pH.
Potentiodynamic polarization and EIS measured corrosion behavior on a Corrtest CS350M workstation.
Statistical modeling quantified factor influence and response prediction.
evidence_signal: The best coating reached a corrosion current density of 2.18 µA cm⁻2 and a corrosion rate of 0.010 mm yr⁻1, with model fits reported above R² = 0.99.
watch_item: The reported performance is based on controlled electrochemical testing, so real-world durability under long-term mechanical wear and cycling still needs validation.
paper_title: STUDY ON THE CORROSION BEHAVIOUR OF PLASMA SPRAYED NANO Al2O3–TiO2– AISI 1020 STEEL COATINGS USING DEFINITIVE SCREENING DESIGN
